Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-7343-RSHD-P2WN-00000-00AXENS S.A. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH BASF SE AND OTHERS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:11ZLexisNexisSTOCKAXENS S.A. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH BASF SE AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: AXENS S.A.

PermID4296138916Websitehttp://www.axens.netIndustryChemicals & Energy Processing

ACTIVITIES:

Axens S.A. engages in developing, manufacturing, and selling catalysts and adsorbents. It focuses on the conversion of oil, coal, natural gas, and biomass to clean fuels; the production and purification of petrochemical intermediates. The company offers guard beds and grading materials. It also provides technology licensing and engineering design; and consulting and software services, such as advanced control, simulators, technical, and economic studies to optimize refining units; and process licensing and performance programs. In addition, the company offers operations support for technical assistance, proprietary equipment, training, and catalyst management; and technical assistance, training, and consulting services. It serves oil refining, petrochemicals/chemicals, gases, alternative fuels, and water markets. The company was founded in 2001 and is based in Rueil-Malmaison, France with additional offices in Princeton, New Jersey; and Houston, Texas. It has manufacturing sites in Savannah, Georgia; Calvert City, Kentucky; and Willow Island, West Virginia. It has operations worldwide. Axens S.A. operates as a subsidiary of IFP Energies nouvelles.

+ AXENS S.A. [3 Press Releases]

+ AXENS S.A. A NEW TEXTILE-TO-TEXTILE RECYCLING PROCESS VALIDATED ON AN INDUSTRIAL SCALE

Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN have completed a recycling loop for polyester textiles. Several tens of tons of post-consumer, polyester-rich, European textile wastes, sorted and prepared in France, have been processed in the Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN semi-industrial demonstration unit, located in Japan, to successfully produce the base monomer of a 100% recycled polyester. This step paves the way for circular polyester loops for the textile industry, in particular sportswear, home furnishings and the luxury sector.

Source: Company Website

+ PRAJ DEMONSTRATES INTEGRATED ETHANOL-TO-JET PATHWAY FOR SUSTAINABLE AVIATION FUEL WITH AXENS JETANOL(TRADEMARK) TECHNOLOGY

30 January, 2026 - Praj Industries, a global leader in bioenergy, technology and sustainable solutions, has successfully demonstrated its Ethanol-to-Jet (EtJ) process utilizing Axens JetanolTM technology at its fully Integrated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) demonstration plant at Praj Matrix, its R&D centre-marking a significant milestone in the global transition to low-carbon aviation fuels. The announcement was made by Mr. Atul Mulay, President - Bionergy at Praj Industries at Wings India 2026, India's premier aviation industry platform in the presence of industry leaders, policymakers, and key stakeholders from the global aviation ecosystem.

The Integrated SAF demo plant, earlier inaugurated by the Hon'ble Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Shri. Hardeep Singh Puri, has now proven its technological readiness for both AtJ pathways. After successful demonstration on bio isobutanol as the feedstock, the plant has now validated bio-ethanol as a feedstock under the EtJ pathway. With this achievement, Praj has further strengthened the versatility and robustness of its feedstock to SAF technology portfolio.

Source: Company Website

+ AXENS CONTINUES ITS PUBLIC CONSULTATION REGARDING ITS CATHODE ACTIVE MATERIAL PRODUCTION PLANT PROJECT

Following the preliminary consultation held from May 5 to July 5, 2025, and the publication of the project owners' report, Axens and RTE (Reseau de Transport d'Electricite - the French Transmission System Operator) have entered into a continuous consultation process to share project-related information with stakeholders and residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut urban communities.

The continuous consultation mechanism implemented by Axens aims to maintain a transparent and constructive dialogue around the project with residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut communities. This is achieved through the reopening of the participatory pages on the consultation website (www.concertation-macaron.fr). The process will make it possible to share and publicly release the results of the various studies and includes the creation of a project monitoring committee composed of stakeholders who expressed particular interest in the project during the preliminary consultation phase.

The National Commission for Public Debate has appointed Ms. Catherine JACQUART as the guarantor of the continuous consultation. The first meeting of the monitoring committee was held on December 16 at the Valenciennes Chamber of Commerce and Industry. It made it possible to present the continuous consultation mechanism as well as several project updates resulting from progress in ongoing studies. The site footprint and layout were further detailed. Technical parameters were also refined: the plant's production capacity is now estimated at 39-43 kt/year, and information related to water consumption as well as the selected cooling system was presented.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BASF SE [3 Press Releases]

+ BASF: INTERNATIONAL COLOR DAY: WHEN AUTOMOTIVE COLOR MEETS PERFORMANCE, PROTECTION AND SUSTAINABILITY

Color significantly influences consumer purchasing decisions, vehicle longevity and brand identity Creating, protecting and preserving automotive colors combines emotional design and materials science BASF Coatings develops more than 1,300 color concepts every year Automotive color is where emotional design meets materials science. It influences brand identity, purchase decisions, safety perception and even sustainability. In anticipation of International Color Day on March 21, BASF Coatings is highlighting the crucial role color plays in automotive design, manufacturing and repair. Far beyond aesthetics, automotive color is a complex, data-driven, and technically precise discipline that requires profound knowledge substrate pre-treatment, the development of different layers that build the final coating and the skill to exactly replicate the status quo of a color shade even years after its application.

Protecting color from the very first layer

With decades of expertise in color innovation, BASF Coatings is well-positioned to create, protect and preserve automotive color across the full lifecycle of a vehicle. When car owners talk about the color of their car, they usually refer to the visible, colorful basecoat only. But to make a color shine, it takes way more than just the pigmentation. BASF Coatings' integrated approach ensures that vehicles also adhere to stringent durability and sustainability standards such as corrosion protection.

"Pretreatment is the invisible enabler behind durable, sustainable automotive coatings. By offering tailored pretreatment solutions for modern automotive substrates, we create the chemical foundation for corrosion resistance and robust paint adhesion. This first process step is decisive for the long-term performance, durability and sustainability of the entire coating system and the basis to make every color shine," said Meike Flock, Head of Global Segment Management Automotive OEM Surface Treatment.

Designing automotive colors for performance and with responsible material choices

The diversity of today's automotive colors is driven by the precise interplay of pigments and effect materials that give each basecoat its distinctive character. At BASF Coatings, this expertise is combined with close collaboration between the global Color Design team and automotive manufacturers, enabling innovative color solutions that meet high performance standards while allowing the integration of more responsible material choices where feasible.

This approach has recently been recognized by leading design and technology institutions. The trend color REVERENCE DARKNESS of BASF Coatings received the MATERIALICA Design and Technology Award 2025, honoring its use of recycled tire materials as a carbon black source. In parallel, BASF Coatings was nominated as a finalist for the German Ecodesign Award 2025 with its project "Colors with Renewable Pigments", demonstrating how biobased

+ BASF AGRICULTURAL SOLUTIONS INVESTS 17 MILLION EURO TO EXPAND ITS CANOLA BREEDING CENTRE OF INNOVATION IN SASKATOON, CANADA

Strategic investment, building on 30 years of global leadership in canola Construction taking place from spring 2026 through end of 2027, adding breeding capacity and advanced automation Facility will enable faster genetic gain and new trait integration, driving greater yields, disease protection and performance for growers BASF Agricultural Solutions is investing 17 million euro to expand and transform its Canola Breeding Centre of Innovation in Saskatoon, Canada, with construction commencing this spring. The investment will strengthen BASF's position as a global leader in canola advancement and reinforce its long-term commitment to deliver leading-edge genetics for canola growers globally.

For 30 years, InVigor® hybrid canola has played a central role in advancing productivity and performance for canola and oilseed rape growers around the world. This investment enhances BASF's breeding capacities, integrating advanced automation, and accelerating the development of new hybrids.

"Canola is a strategic crop for our Agricultural Solutions business, playing a crucial role for Canadian farmers and supporting food production worldwide," said Livio Tedeschi, President, BASF Agricultural Solutions. "This investment underscores our commitment to advancing our entire portfolio of canola. As the global leader in canola production and innovation, BASF is proud to invest in its future innovation capabilities." Accelerating innovation through advanced breeding technologies

A key component of the Saskatoon facility expansion is the development of new infrastructure to support precision-controlled environment growth systems and high throughput automation pipelines. "These enhancements are critical to implementing genomic selection at scale, enabling faster, more precise breeding decisions and accelerating genetic gain across all InVigor programs. By increasing breeding throughput and improving predictive capabilities, BASF can shorten innovation cycles and deliver improved hybrids to the market more efficiently", explained Doreen Schachtschabel, Senior Vice President, R&D for Seeds & Traits.

The expansion will also include a new advanced research-grade glasshouse designed to support the advancement of future hybrid breeding programs. By expanding its research capabilities in Canada, BASF is strengthening its ability to deliver differentiated solutions that can drive greater yields, improve disease protection and offer overall enhanced performance for farmers.

+ BASF INCREASES PRICES FOR NEOPENTYL GLYCOL IN EUROPE

With immediate effect, or as existing contracts permit, BASF is increasing the prices of the polyalcohol neopentyl glycol (Neol®) in Europe:

Product :Neopentyl glycol

Price Increase + 350 Euro (MT)

Neopentyl glycol is a high-quality intermediate used, for example, to produce polyester resins for coatings, unsaturated polyester resins, lubricants and plasticizers.

+ HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC. [1 Press Release]

+ TUSKEGEE UNIVERSITY UPGRADES CAMPUS SAFETY SYSTEMS WITH HONEYWELL AI ENABLED SMART ACCESS TECHNOLOGY

CHARLOTTE, N.C., Nov. 18, 2025 -- Tuskegee University, one of the nation's most respected Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), today announced a multi-year, strategic project to implement a fully integrated security system across its campus using Honeywell's (NASDAQ: HON) AI-enabled smart access technology. The upgrades will aim to further strengthen Tuskegee University's commitment to the safety of its students, faculty, staff and visitors.

Tuskegee University will utilize Honeywell's state-of-the-art commercial security solutions to deploy centralized access control and real-time monitoring tools, helping improve incident response times and reduce operational complexity for security teams. As higher education institutions nationwide face mounting pressure to enhance security and adapt to new technologies, these upgrades aim to position the University as a leader in on-campus safety.

Tuskegee University will leverage several of Honeywell's Building Automation solutions. Key features include:

+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

DASHBOARD: AXENS S.A.

PermID4296138916Websitehttp://www.axens.netIndustryChemicals & Energy Processing

ACTIVITIES:

Axens S.A. engages in developing, manufacturing, and selling catalysts and adsorbents. It focuses on the conversion of oil, coal, natural gas, and biomass to clean fuels; the production and purification of petrochemical intermediates. The company offers guard beds and grading materials. It also provides technology licensing and engineering design; and consulting and software services, such as advanced control, simulators, technical, and economic studies to optimize refining units; and process licensing and performance programs. In addition, the company offers operations support for technical assistance, proprietary equipment, training, and catalyst management; and technical assistance, training, and consulting services. It serves oil refining, petrochemicals/chemicals, gases, alternative fuels, and water markets. The company was founded in 2001 and is based in Rueil-Malmaison, France with additional offices in Princeton, New Jersey; and Houston, Texas. It has manufacturing sites in Savannah, Georgia; Calvert City, Kentucky; and Willow Island, West Virginia. It has operations worldwide. Axens S.A. operates as a subsidiary of IFP Energies nouvelles.

+ AXENS S.A. [3 Press Releases]

+ AXENS S.A. A NEW TEXTILE-TO-TEXTILE RECYCLING PROCESS VALIDATED ON AN INDUSTRIAL SCALE

Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN have completed a recycling loop for polyester textiles. Several tens of tons of post-consumer, polyester-rich, European textile wastes, sorted and prepared in France, have been processed in the Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN semi-industrial demonstration unit, located in Japan, to successfully produce the base monomer of a 100% recycled polyester. This step paves the way for circular polyester loops for the textile industry, in particular sportswear, home furnishings and the luxury sector.

+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JJW-3NN3-RSP0-R48S-00000-00AXENS S.A. - UPDATES FROM BASF SE AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:11ZLexisNexisSTOCKAXENS S.A. - UPDATES FROM BASF SE AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: AXENS S.A.

PermID4296138916Websitehttp://www.axens.netIndustryChemicals & Energy Processing

ACTIVITIES:

Axens S.A. engages in developing, manufacturing, and selling catalysts and adsorbents. It focuses on the conversion of oil, coal, natural gas, and biomass to clean fuels; the production and purification of petrochemical intermediates. The company offers guard beds and grading materials. It also provides technology licensing and engineering design; and consulting and software services, such as advanced control, simulators, technical, and economic studies to optimize refining units; and process licensing and performance programs. In addition, the company offers operations support for technical assistance, proprietary equipment, training, and catalyst management; and technical assistance, training, and consulting services. It serves oil refining, petrochemicals/chemicals, gases, alternative fuels, and water markets. The company was founded in 2001 and is based in Rueil-Malmaison, France with additional offices in Princeton, New Jersey; and Houston, Texas. It has manufacturing sites in Savannah, Georgia; Calvert City, Kentucky; and Willow Island, West Virginia. It has operations worldwide. Axens S.A. operates as a subsidiary of IFP Energies nouvelles.

+ AXENS S.A. [3 Press Releases]

+ AXENS S.A. A NEW TEXTILE-TO-TEXTILE RECYCLING PROCESS VALIDATED ON AN INDUSTRIAL SCALE

Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN have completed a recycling loop for polyester textiles. Several tens of tons of post-consumer, polyester-rich, European textile wastes, sorted and prepared in France, have been processed in the Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN semi-industrial demonstration unit, located in Japan, to successfully produce the base monomer of a 100% recycled polyester. This step paves the way for circular polyester loops for the textile industry, in particular sportswear, home furnishings and the luxury sector.

Source: Company Website

+ PRAJ DEMONSTRATES INTEGRATED ETHANOL-TO-JET PATHWAY FOR SUSTAINABLE AVIATION FUEL WITH AXENS JETANOL(TRADEMARK) TECHNOLOGY

30 January, 2026 - Praj Industries, a global leader in bioenergy, technology and sustainable solutions, has successfully demonstrated its Ethanol-to-Jet (EtJ) process utilizing Axens JetanolTM technology at its fully Integrated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) demonstration plant at Praj Matrix, its R&D centre-marking a significant milestone in the global transition to low-carbon aviation fuels. The announcement was made by Mr. Atul Mulay, President - Bionergy at Praj Industries at Wings India 2026, India's premier aviation industry platform in the presence of industry leaders, policymakers, and key stakeholders from the global aviation ecosystem.

The Integrated SAF demo plant, earlier inaugurated by the Hon'ble Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Shri. Hardeep Singh Puri, has now proven its technological readiness for both AtJ pathways. After successful demonstration on bio isobutanol as the feedstock, the plant has now validated bio-ethanol as a feedstock under the EtJ pathway. With this achievement, Praj has further strengthened the versatility and robustness of its feedstock to SAF technology portfolio.

Source: Company Website

+ AXENS CONTINUES ITS PUBLIC CONSULTATION REGARDING ITS CATHODE ACTIVE MATERIAL PRODUCTION PLANT PROJECT

Following the preliminary consultation held from May 5 to July 5, 2025, and the publication of the project owners' report, Axens and RTE (Reseau de Transport d'Electricite - the French Transmission System Operator) have entered into a continuous consultation process to share project-related information with stakeholders and residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut urban communities.

The continuous consultation mechanism implemented by Axens aims to maintain a transparent and constructive dialogue around the project with residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut communities. This is achieved through the reopening of the participatory pages on the consultation website (www.concertation-macaron.fr). The process will make it possible to share and publicly release the results of the various studies and includes the creation of a project monitoring committee composed of stakeholders who expressed particular interest in the project during the preliminary consultation phase.

The National Commission for Public Debate has appointed Ms. Catherine JACQUART as the guarantor of the continuous consultation. The first meeting of the monitoring committee was held on December 16 at the Valenciennes Chamber of Commerce and Industry. It made it possible to present the continuous consultation mechanism as well as several project updates resulting from progress in ongoing studies. The site footprint and layout were further detailed. Technical parameters were also refined: the plant's production capacity is now estimated at 39-43 kt/year, and information related to water consumption as well as the selected cooling system was presented.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BASF SE [3 Press Releases]

+ BASF: INTERNATIONAL COLOR DAY: WHEN AUTOMOTIVE COLOR MEETS PERFORMANCE, PROTECTION AND SUSTAINABILITY

Color significantly influences consumer purchasing decisions, vehicle longevity and brand identity Creating, protecting and preserving automotive colors combines emotional design and materials science BASF Coatings develops more than 1,300 color concepts every year Automotive color is where emotional design meets materials science. It influences brand identity, purchase decisions, safety perception and even sustainability. In anticipation of International Color Day on March 21, BASF Coatings is highlighting the crucial role color plays in automotive design, manufacturing and repair. Far beyond aesthetics, automotive color is a complex, data-driven, and technically precise discipline that requires profound knowledge substrate pre-treatment, the development of different layers that build the final coating and the skill to exactly replicate the status quo of a color shade even years after its application.

Protecting color from the very first layer

With decades of expertise in color innovation, BASF Coatings is well-positioned to create, protect and preserve automotive color across the full lifecycle of a vehicle. When car owners talk about the color of their car, they usually refer to the visible, colorful basecoat only. But to make a color shine, it takes way more than just the pigmentation. BASF Coatings' integrated approach ensures that vehicles also adhere to stringent durability and sustainability standards such as corrosion protection.

"Pretreatment is the invisible enabler behind durable, sustainable automotive coatings. By offering tailored pretreatment solutions for modern automotive substrates, we create the chemical foundation for corrosion resistance and robust paint adhesion. This first process step is decisive for the long-term performance, durability and sustainability of the entire coating system and the basis to make every color shine," said Meike Flock, Head of Global Segment Management Automotive OEM Surface Treatment.

Designing automotive colors for performance and with responsible material choices

The diversity of today's automotive colors is driven by the precise interplay of pigments and effect materials that give each basecoat its distinctive character. At BASF Coatings, this expertise is combined with close collaboration between the global Color Design team and automotive manufacturers, enabling innovative color solutions that meet high performance standards while allowing the integration of more responsible material choices where feasible.

This approach has recently been recognized by leading design and technology institutions. The trend color REVERENCE DARKNESS of BASF Coatings received the MATERIALICA Design and Technology Award 2025, honoring its use of recycled tire materials as a carbon black source. In parallel, BASF Coatings was nominated as a finalist for the German Ecodesign Award 2025 with its project "Colors with Renewable Pigments", demonstrating how biobased

+ BASF AGRICULTURAL SOLUTIONS INVESTS 17 MILLION EURO TO EXPAND ITS CANOLA BREEDING CENTRE OF INNOVATION IN SASKATOON, CANADA

Strategic investment, building on 30 years of global leadership in canola Construction taking place from spring 2026 through end of 2027, adding breeding capacity and advanced automation Facility will enable faster genetic gain and new trait integration, driving greater yields, disease protection and performance for growers BASF Agricultural Solutions is investing 17 million euro to expand and transform its Canola Breeding Centre of Innovation in Saskatoon, Canada, with construction commencing this spring. The investment will strengthen BASF's position as a global leader in canola advancement and reinforce its long-term commitment to deliver leading-edge genetics for canola growers globally.

For 30 years, InVigor® hybrid canola has played a central role in advancing productivity and performance for canola and oilseed rape growers around the world. This investment enhances BASF's breeding capacities, integrating advanced automation, and accelerating the development of new hybrids.

"Canola is a strategic crop for our Agricultural Solutions business, playing a crucial role for Canadian farmers and supporting food production worldwide," said Livio Tedeschi, President, BASF Agricultural Solutions. "This investment underscores our commitment to advancing our entire portfolio of canola. As the global leader in canola production and innovation, BASF is proud to invest in its future innovation capabilities." Accelerating innovation through advanced breeding technologies

A key component of the Saskatoon facility expansion is the development of new infrastructure to support precision-controlled environment growth systems and high throughput automation pipelines. "These enhancements are critical to implementing genomic selection at scale, enabling faster, more precise breeding decisions and accelerating genetic gain across all InVigor programs. By increasing breeding throughput and improving predictive capabilities, BASF can shorten innovation cycles and deliver improved hybrids to the market more efficiently", explained Doreen Schachtschabel, Senior Vice President, R&D for Seeds & Traits.

The expansion will also include a new advanced research-grade glasshouse designed to support the advancement of future hybrid breeding programs. By expanding its research capabilities in Canada, BASF is strengthening its ability to deliver differentiated solutions that can drive greater yields, improve disease protection and offer overall enhanced performance for farmers.

+ BASF INCREASES PRICES FOR NEOPENTYL GLYCOL IN EUROPE

With immediate effect, or as existing contracts permit, BASF is increasing the prices of the polyalcohol neopentyl glycol (Neol®) in Europe:

Product :Neopentyl glycol

Price Increase + 350 Euro (MT)

Neopentyl glycol is a high-quality intermediate used, for example, to produce polyester resins for coatings, unsaturated polyester resins, lubricants and plasticizers.

+ HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC. [1 Press Release]

+ TUSKEGEE UNIVERSITY UPGRADES CAMPUS SAFETY SYSTEMS WITH HONEYWELL AI ENABLED SMART ACCESS TECHNOLOGY

CHARLOTTE, N.C., Nov. 18, 2025 -- Tuskegee University, one of the nation's most respected Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), today announced a multi-year, strategic project to implement a fully integrated security system across its campus using Honeywell's (NASDAQ: HON) AI-enabled smart access technology. The upgrades will aim to further strengthen Tuskegee University's commitment to the safety of its students, faculty, staff and visitors.

Tuskegee University will utilize Honeywell's state-of-the-art commercial security solutions to deploy centralized access control and real-time monitoring tools, helping improve incident response times and reduce operational complexity for security teams. As higher education institutions nationwide face mounting pressure to enhance security and adapt to new technologies, these upgrades aim to position the University as a leader in on-campus safety.

urn:contentItem:6JJP-8M03-RRXR-B37H-00000-00AXENS S.A. - THE RECENT NEWS FROM BASF SE AND OTHER PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:11ZLexisNexisSTOCKAXENS S.A. - THE RECENT NEWS FROM BASF SE AND OTHER PEERS

DASHBOARD: AXENS S.A.

PermID4296138916Websitehttp://www.axens.netIndustryChemicals & Energy Processing

ACTIVITIES:

Axens S.A. engages in developing, manufacturing, and selling catalysts and adsorbents. It focuses on the conversion of oil, coal, natural gas, and biomass to clean fuels; the production and purification of petrochemical intermediates. The company offers guard beds and grading materials. It also provides technology licensing and engineering design; and consulting and software services, such as advanced control, simulators, technical, and economic studies to optimize refining units; and process licensing and performance programs. In addition, the company offers operations support for technical assistance, proprietary equipment, training, and catalyst management; and technical assistance, training, and consulting services. It serves oil refining, petrochemicals/chemicals, gases, alternative fuels, and water markets. The company was founded in 2001 and is based in Rueil-Malmaison, France with additional offices in Princeton, New Jersey; and Houston, Texas. It has manufacturing sites in Savannah, Georgia; Calvert City, Kentucky; and Willow Island, West Virginia. It has operations worldwide. Axens S.A. operates as a subsidiary of IFP Energies nouvelles.

+ AXENS S.A. [3 Press Releases]

+ AXENS S.A. A NEW TEXTILE-TO-TEXTILE RECYCLING PROCESS VALIDATED ON AN INDUSTRIAL SCALE

Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN have completed a recycling loop for polyester textiles. Several tens of tons of post-consumer, polyester-rich, European textile wastes, sorted and prepared in France, have been processed in the Axens, IFPEN and JEPLAN semi-industrial demonstration unit, located in Japan, to successfully produce the base monomer of a 100% recycled polyester. This step paves the way for circular polyester loops for the textile industry, in particular sportswear, home furnishings and the luxury sector.

Source: Company Website

+ PRAJ DEMONSTRATES INTEGRATED ETHANOL-TO-JET PATHWAY FOR SUSTAINABLE AVIATION FUEL WITH AXENS JETANOL(TRADEMARK) TECHNOLOGY

30 January, 2026 - Praj Industries, a global leader in bioenergy, technology and sustainable solutions, has successfully demonstrated its Ethanol-to-Jet (EtJ) process utilizing Axens JetanolTM technology at its fully Integrated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) demonstration plant at Praj Matrix, its R&D centre-marking a significant milestone in the global transition to low-carbon aviation fuels. The announcement was made by Mr. Atul Mulay, President - Bionergy at Praj Industries at Wings India 2026, India's premier aviation industry platform in the presence of industry leaders, policymakers, and key stakeholders from the global aviation ecosystem.

The Integrated SAF demo plant, earlier inaugurated by the Hon'ble Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Shri. Hardeep Singh Puri, has now proven its technological readiness for both AtJ pathways. After successful demonstration on bio isobutanol as the feedstock, the plant has now validated bio-ethanol as a feedstock under the EtJ pathway. With this achievement, Praj has further strengthened the versatility and robustness of its feedstock to SAF technology portfolio.

Source: Company Website

+ AXENS CONTINUES ITS PUBLIC CONSULTATION REGARDING ITS CATHODE ACTIVE MATERIAL PRODUCTION PLANT PROJECT

Following the preliminary consultation held from May 5 to July 5, 2025, and the publication of the project owners' report, Axens and RTE (Reseau de Transport d'Electricite - the French Transmission System Operator) have entered into a continuous consultation process to share project-related information with stakeholders and residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut urban communities.

The continuous consultation mechanism implemented by Axens aims to maintain a transparent and constructive dialogue around the project with residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut communities. This is achieved through the reopening of the participatory pages on the consultation website (www.concertation-macaron.fr). The process will make it possible to share and publicly release the results of the various studies and includes the creation of a project monitoring committee composed of stakeholders who expressed particular interest in the project during the preliminary consultation phase.

The National Commission for Public Debate has appointed Ms. Catherine JACQUART as the guarantor of the continuous consultation. The first meeting of the monitoring committee was held on December 16 at the Valenciennes Chamber of Commerce and Industry. It made it possible to present the continuous consultation mechanism as well as several project updates resulting from progress in ongoing studies. The site footprint and layout were further detailed. Technical parameters were also refined: the plant's production capacity is now estimated at 39-43 kt/year, and information related to water consumption as well as the selected cooling system was presented.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BASF SE [3 Press Releases]

+ BASF: INTERNATIONAL COLOR DAY: WHEN AUTOMOTIVE COLOR MEETS PERFORMANCE, PROTECTION AND SUSTAINABILITY

Color significantly influences consumer purchasing decisions, vehicle longevity and brand identity Creating, protecting and preserving automotive colors combines emotional design and materials science BASF Coatings develops more than 1,300 color concepts every year Automotive color is where emotional design meets materials science. It influences brand identity, purchase decisions, safety perception and even sustainability. In anticipation of International Color Day on March 21, BASF Coatings is highlighting the crucial role color plays in automotive design, manufacturing and repair. Far beyond aesthetics, automotive color is a complex, data-driven, and technically precise discipline that requires profound knowledge substrate pre-treatment, the development of different layers that build the final coating and the skill to exactly replicate the status quo of a color shade even years after its application.

Protecting color from the very first layer

With decades of expertise in color innovation, BASF Coatings is well-positioned to create, protect and preserve automotive color across the full lifecycle of a vehicle. When car owners talk about the color of their car, they usually refer to the visible, colorful basecoat only. But to make a color shine, it takes way more than just the pigmentation. BASF Coatings' integrated approach ensures that vehicles also adhere to stringent durability and sustainability standards such as corrosion protection.

"Pretreatment is the invisible enabler behind durable, sustainable automotive coatings. By offering tailored pretreatment solutions for modern automotive substrates, we create the chemical foundation for corrosion resistance and robust paint adhesion. This first process step is decisive for the long-term performance, durability and sustainability of the entire coating system and the basis to make every color shine," said Meike Flock, Head of Global Segment Management Automotive OEM Surface Treatment.

Designing automotive colors for performance and with responsible material choices

The diversity of today's automotive colors is driven by the precise interplay of pigments and effect materials that give each basecoat its distinctive character. At BASF Coatings, this expertise is combined with close collaboration between the global Color Design team and automotive manufacturers, enabling innovative color solutions that meet high performance standards while allowing the integration of more responsible material choices where feasible.

This approach has recently been recognized by leading design and technology institutions. The trend color REVERENCE DARKNESS of BASF Coatings received the MATERIALICA Design and Technology Award 2025, honoring its use of recycled tire materials as a carbon black source. In parallel, BASF Coatings was nominated as a finalist for the German Ecodesign Award 2025 with its project "Colors with Renewable Pigments", demonstrating how biobased

+ BASF AGRICULTURAL SOLUTIONS INVESTS 17 MILLION EURO TO EXPAND ITS CANOLA BREEDING CENTRE OF INNOVATION IN SASKATOON, CANADA

Strategic investment, building on 30 years of global leadership in canola Construction taking place from spring 2026 through end of 2027, adding breeding capacity and advanced automation Facility will enable faster genetic gain and new trait integration, driving greater yields, disease protection and performance for growers BASF Agricultural Solutions is investing 17 million euro to expand and transform its Canola Breeding Centre of Innovation in Saskatoon, Canada, with construction commencing this spring. The investment will strengthen BASF's position as a global leader in canola advancement and reinforce its long-term commitment to deliver leading-edge genetics for canola growers globally.

For 30 years, InVigor® hybrid canola has played a central role in advancing productivity and performance for canola and oilseed rape growers around the world. This investment enhances BASF's breeding capacities, integrating advanced automation, and accelerating the development of new hybrids.

"Canola is a strategic crop for our Agricultural Solutions business, playing a crucial role for Canadian farmers and supporting food production worldwide," said Livio Tedeschi, President, BASF Agricultural Solutions. "This investment underscores our commitment to advancing our entire portfolio of canola. As the global leader in canola production and innovation, BASF is proud to invest in its future innovation capabilities." Accelerating innovation through advanced breeding technologies

A key component of the Saskatoon facility expansion is the development of new infrastructure to support precision-controlled environment growth systems and high throughput automation pipelines. "These enhancements are critical to implementing genomic selection at scale, enabling faster, more precise breeding decisions and accelerating genetic gain across all InVigor programs. By increasing breeding throughput and improving predictive capabilities, BASF can shorten innovation cycles and deliver improved hybrids to the market more efficiently", explained Doreen Schachtschabel, Senior Vice President, R&D for Seeds & Traits.

The expansion will also include a new advanced research-grade glasshouse designed to support the advancement of future hybrid breeding programs. By expanding its research capabilities in Canada, BASF is strengthening its ability to deliver differentiated solutions that can drive greater yields, improve disease protection and offer overall enhanced performance for farmers.

+ BASF INCREASES PRICES FOR NEOPENTYL GLYCOL IN EUROPE

With immediate effect, or as existing contracts permit, BASF is increasing the prices of the polyalcohol neopentyl glycol (Neol®) in Europe:

Product :Neopentyl glycol

Price Increase + 350 Euro (MT)

Neopentyl glycol is a high-quality intermediate used, for example, to produce polyester resins for coatings, unsaturated polyester resins, lubricants and plasticizers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC. [1 Press Release]

+ TUSKEGEE UNIVERSITY UPGRADES CAMPUS SAFETY SYSTEMS WITH HONEYWELL AI ENABLED SMART ACCESS TECHNOLOGY

CHARLOTTE, N.C., Nov. 18, 2025 -- Tuskegee University, one of the nation's most respected Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), today announced a multi-year, strategic project to implement a fully integrated security system across its campus using Honeywell's (NASDAQ: HON) AI-enabled smart access technology. The upgrades will aim to further strengthen Tuskegee University's commitment to the safety of its students, faculty, staff and visitors.

Tuskegee University will utilize Honeywell's state-of-the-art commercial security solutions to deploy centralized access control and real-time monitoring tools, helping improve incident response times and reduce operational complexity for security teams. As higher education institutions nationwide face mounting pressure to enhance security and adapt to new technologies, these upgrades aim to position the University as a leader in on-campus safety.

Tuskegee University will leverage several of Honeywell's Building Automation solutions. Key features include:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

This is achieved through the reopening of the participatory pages on the consultation website (www.concertation-macaron.fr). The process will make it possible to share and publicly release the results of the various studies and includes the creation of a project monitoring committee composed of stakeholders who expressed particular interest in the project during the preliminary consultation phase.

The National Commission for Public Debate has appointed Ms. Catherine JACQUART as the guarantor of the continuous consultation. The first meeting of the monitoring committee was held on December 16 at the Valenciennes Chamber of Commerce and Industry. It made it possible to present the continuous consultation mechanism as well as several project updates resulting from progress in ongoing studies. The site footprint and layout were further detailed. Technical parameters were also refined: the plant's production capacity is now estimated at 39-43 kt/year, and information related to water consumption as well as the selected cooling system was presented.

Source: Company Website

+ BASF SE [3 Press Releases]

+ BASF: INTERNATIONAL COLOR DAY: WHEN AUTOMOTIVE COLOR MEETS PERFORMANCE, PROTECTION AND SUSTAINABILITY

Color significantly influences consumer purchasing decisions, vehicle longevity and brand identity Creating, protecting and preserving automotive colors combines emotional design and materials science BASF Coatings develops more than 1,300 color concepts every year Automotive color is where emotional design meets materials science. It influences brand identity, purchase decisions, safety perception and even sustainability. In anticipation of International Color Day on March 21, BASF Coatings is highlighting the crucial role color plays in automotive design, manufacturing and repair. Far beyond aesthetics, automotive color is a complex, data-driven, and technically precise discipline that requires profound knowledge substrate pre-treatment, the development of different layers that build the final coating and the skill to exactly replicate the status quo of a color shade even years after its application.

Protecting color from the very first layer

With decades of expertise in color innovation, BASF Coatings is well-positioned to create, protect and preserve automotive color across the full lifecycle of a vehicle. When car owners talk about the color of their car, they usually refer to the visible, colorful basecoat only. But to make a color shine, it takes way more than just the pigmentation. BASF Coatings' integrated approach ensures that vehicles also adhere to stringent durability and sustainability standards such as corrosion protection.

"Pretreatment is the invisible enabler behind durable, sustainable automotive coatings. By offering tailored pretreatment solutions for modern automotive substrates, we create the chemical foundation for corrosion resistance and robust paint adhesion. This first process step is decisive for the long-term performance, durability and sustainability of the entire coating system and the basis to make every color shine," said Meike Flock, Head of Global Segment Management Automotive OEM Surface Treatment.

Designing automotive colors for performance and with responsible material choices

The diversity of today's automotive colors is driven by the precise interplay of pigments and effect materials that give each basecoat its distinctive character. At BASF Coatings, this expertise is combined with close collaboration between the global Color Design team and automotive manufacturers, enabling innovative color solutions that meet high performance standards while allowing the integration of more responsible material choices where feasible.

This approach has recently been recognized by leading design and technology institutions. The trend color REVERENCE DARKNESS of BASF Coatings received the MATERIALICA Design and Technology Award 2025, honoring its use of recycled tire materials as a carbon black source. In parallel, BASF Coatings was nominated as a finalist for the German Ecodesign Award 2025 with its project "Colors with Renewable Pigments", demonstrating how biobased

+ BASF AGRICULTURAL SOLUTIONS INVESTS 17 MILLION EURO TO EXPAND ITS CANOLA BREEDING CENTRE OF INNOVATION IN SASKATOON, CANADA

Strategic investment, building on 30 years of global leadership in canola Construction taking place from spring 2026 through end of 2027, adding breeding capacity and advanced automation Facility will enable faster genetic gain and new trait integration, driving greater yields, disease protection and performance for growers BASF Agricultural Solutions is investing 17 million euro to expand and transform its Canola Breeding Centre of Innovation in Saskatoon, Canada, with construction commencing this spring. The investment will strengthen BASF's position as a global leader in canola advancement and reinforce its long-term commitment to deliver leading-edge genetics for canola growers globally.

For 30 years, InVigor® hybrid canola has played a central role in advancing productivity and performance for canola and oilseed rape growers around the world. This investment enhances BASF's breeding capacities, integrating advanced automation, and accelerating the development of new hybrids.

"Canola is a strategic crop for our Agricultural Solutions business, playing a crucial role for Canadian farmers and supporting food production worldwide," said Livio Tedeschi, President, BASF Agricultural Solutions. "This investment underscores our commitment to advancing our entire portfolio of canola. As the global leader in canola production and innovation, BASF is proud to invest in its future innovation capabilities." Accelerating innovation through advanced breeding technologies

A key component of the Saskatoon facility expansion is the development of new infrastructure to support precision-controlled environment growth systems and high throughput automation pipelines. "These enhancements are critical to implementing genomic selection at scale, enabling faster, more precise breeding decisions and accelerating genetic gain across all InVigor programs. By increasing breeding throughput and improving predictive capabilities, BASF can shorten innovation cycles and deliver improved hybrids to the market more efficiently", explained Doreen Schachtschabel, Senior Vice President, R&D for Seeds & Traits.

The expansion will also include a new advanced research-grade glasshouse designed to support the advancement of future hybrid breeding programs. By expanding its research capabilities in Canada, BASF is strengthening its ability to deliver differentiated solutions that can drive greater yields, improve disease protection and offer overall enhanced performance for farmers.

+ BASF INCREASES PRICES FOR NEOPENTYL GLYCOL IN EUROPE

With immediate effect, or as existing contracts permit, BASF is increasing the prices of the polyalcohol neopentyl glycol (Neol®) in Europe:

Product :Neopentyl glycol

Price Increase + 350 Euro (MT)

Neopentyl glycol is a high-quality intermediate used, for example, to produce polyester resins for coatings, unsaturated polyester resins, lubricants and plasticizers.

+ TUSKEGEE UNIVERSITY UPGRADES CAMPUS SAFETY SYSTEMS WITH HONEYWELL AI ENABLED SMART ACCESS TECHNOLOGY

CHARLOTTE, N.C., Nov. 18, 2025 -- Tuskegee University, one of the nation's most respected Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), today announced a multi-year, strategic project to implement a fully integrated security system across its campus using Honeywell's (NASDAQ: HON) AI-enabled smart access technology. The upgrades will aim to further strengthen Tuskegee University's commitment to the safety of its students, faculty, staff and visitors.

Tuskegee University will utilize Honeywell's state-of-the-art commercial security solutions to deploy centralized access control and real-time monitoring tools, helping improve incident response times and reduce operational complexity for security teams. As higher education institutions nationwide face mounting pressure to enhance security and adapt to new technologies, these upgrades aim to position the University as a leader in on-campus safety.

+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JGH-RVD3-RS9K-G472-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Southern Company reports 226% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 02 May 2026 04:34 EDT Release Date: April 30, 20262026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:11ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Southern Company reports 226% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 02 May 2026 04:34 EDT Release Date: April 30, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Southern Company (NYSE:SO), announced net profit of $1.4b for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 226% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 1.6% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Total revenue was up 8% from $7.8b to $8.4b

Unfavourable Changes:

- EBITDA to Revenues was down from 36.5% to 35.6%

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 19.9% to 18.6%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 19.9% to 18.6%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 17.2% to 16.1%

Note:

- Net profit was steady at $1.4b

- EPS was steady at $1.21.

- Depreciation to Revenues was up from 16.5% to 16.9%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

ACTIVITIES:

The Southern Company, a prominent energy holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading provider of electricity and natural gas services in the southeastern United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electric power, serving approximately nine million customers through subsidiaries like Alabama Power, Georgia Power, and Mississippi Power. The company also engages in natural gas distribution and owns interests in nuclear and fossil fuel plants, emphasizing reliable and affordable energy solutions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar and wind projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, investments in modernizing transmission infrastructure and expanding natural gas operations are fueling growth, positioning the company to capitalize on the transition to a more sustainable energy landscape while maintaining operational efficiency and regulatory compliance.

MARKET POSITION:

The Southern Company (NYSE:SO), the NYSE's 3rd largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, climbed $8.16 (or 9.2%) in the trailing 12 months to May 01, 2026 to close at $96.71. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 29.8% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -20.6%. Average daily volume of 5.4 million shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JGP-35J3-RSH5-M0KF-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Southern Company reports 20% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:11ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Southern Company reports 20% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue

AMERICAN RESULTS

Southern Company (NYSE:SO), announced total revenue of $8.4b for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 20% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 8% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Total revenue was up 8% from $7.8b to $8.4b

Unfavourable Changes:

- EBITDA to Revenues was down from 36.5% to 35.6%

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 19.9% to 18.6%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 19.9% to 18.6%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 17.2% to 16.1%

Note:

- Net profit was steady at $1.4b

- EPS was steady at $1.21.

- Depreciation to Revenues was up from 16.5% to 16.9%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, c121-155Revenue, $ billion8.47.07.8Sequential growth in Revenue %20.3-10.812.2Net Profit, $1.4 billion416 million1.7 billionSequential growth in Net Profit %226-7694

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] Revenue was up 8% and Net Profit was up 1.6%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, c121121PCP growth in EPS %-17Revenue, $ billion8.47.8PCP growth in Revenue %8.017.0Net Profit, $ billion1.41.3PCP growth in Net Profit %1.618.2

ACTIVITIES:

The Southern Company, a prominent energy holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading provider of electricity and natural gas services in the southeastern United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electric power, serving approximately nine million customers through subsidiaries like Alabama Power, Georgia Power, and Mississippi Power. The company also engages in natural gas distribution and owns interests in nuclear and fossil fuel plants, emphasizing reliable and affordable energy solutions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar and wind projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, investments in modernizing transmission infrastructure and expanding natural gas operations are fueling growth, positioning the company to capitalize on the transition to a more sustainable energy landscape while maintaining operational efficiency and regulatory compliance.

MARKET POSITION:

The Southern Company (NYSE:SO), the NYSE's 3rd largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, climbed $8.16 (or 9.2%) in the trailing 12 months to May 01, 2026 to close at $96.71. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 29.8% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -20.6%. Average daily volume of 5.4 million shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JGB-F083-RT76-J10P-00000-00Financial Review: Polar Power NASDAQ:POLA and Southern NYSE:SO2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:11ZLexisNexisFinancial Review: Polar Power NASDAQ:POLA and Southern NYSE:SO

Southern NYSE:SO - Get Free Report and Polar Power NASDAQ:POLA - Get Free Report are both utilities companies, but which is the better stock? We will compare the two businesses based on the strength of their dividends, institutional ownership, risk, valuation, earnings, profitability and analyst recommendations.

Profitability

This table compares Southern and Polar Power's net margins, return on equity and return on assets. Net MarginsReturn on EquityReturn on AssetsSouthern14.69%12.52%3.13%Polar Power-144.88%-211.39%-65.33%

Institutional Insider Ownership

64.1% of Southern shares are held by institutional investors. Comparatively, 26.1% of Polar Power shares are held by institutional investors. 0.2% of Southern shares are held by insiders. Comparatively, 30.8% of Polar Power shares are held by insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that hedge funds, endowments and large money managers believe a stock is poised for long-term growth.

Risk and Volatility

Southern has a beta of 0.41, meaning that its share price is 59% less volatile than the S 500. Comparatively, Polar Power has a beta of 1.17, meaning that its share price is 17% more volatile than the S 500.

Analyst Recommendations

This is a summary of recent ratings and target prices for Southern and Polar Power, as reported by MarketBeat. Sell RatingsHold RatingsBuy RatingsStrong Buy RatingsRating ScoreSouthern113702.29Polar Power10001.00

Southern presently has a consensus target price of $99.08, suggesting a potential upside of 2.51%. Given Southern's stronger consensus rating and higher possible upside, analysts clearly believe Southern is more favorable than Polar Power.

Valuation Earnings

This table compares Southern and Polar Power"s revenue, earnings per share EPS and valuation. Gross RevenuePrice/Sales RatioNet IncomeEarnings Per SharePrice/Earnings RatioSouthern$29.55 billion3.69$4.34 billion$3.9324.59Polar Power$6.30 million1.05-$9.13 million$3.59-0.51

Southern has higher revenue and earnings than Polar Power. Polar Power is trading at a lower price-to-earnings ratio than Southern, indicating that it is currently the more affordable of the two stocks.

Summary

Southern beats Polar Power on 12 of the 14 factors compared between the two stocks.

About Southern Get Free Report

The Southern Company, through its subsidiaries, engages in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. The company also develops, constructs, acquires, owns, and manages power generation assets, including renewable energy projects and sells electricity in the wholesale market; and distributes natural gas in Illinois, Georgia, Virginia, and Tennessee, as well as provides gas marketing services, gas distribution operations, and gas pipeline investments operations. In addition, it owns and operates nuclear, coal, hydro, cogeneration, solar, wind, battery storage, and fuel cell facilities. Further, the constructs, operates, and maintains approximately 77,900 miles of natural gas pipelines and 14 storage facilities with total capacity of 157 Bcf to provide natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers. The company serves approximately 8.9 million electric and gas utility customers. Further, it develops distributed energy and resilience solutions; deploys microgrids for commercial, industrial, governmental, and utility customers; and offers digital wireless communications and fiber optics services. The Southern Company was incorporated in 1945 and is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.

About Polar Power Get Free Report

Polar Power, Inc. designs, manufactures, and sells direct current DC power generators, renewable energy, and cooling systems in the United States and internationally. The company offers DC base power systems, DC hybrid power systems, DC solar hybrid power systems, and mobile power systems. Its DC power systems are available in diesel, natural gas, LPG/propane, and renewable fuel formats. It serves customers in the telecommunications, military, commercial, industrial, and marine markets. In addition, the company sells its products through a direct sales force, and independent service providers and dealers. The company was formerly known as Polar Products, Inc. and changed its name to Polar Power, Inc. in October 1991. Polar Power, Inc. was incorporated in 1979 and is based in Gardena, California.

urn:contentItem:6JJN-M7W3-S8VK-W2Y4-00000-00Can The Southern Company (NYSE:SO), sustain its upward momentum this Tuesday?2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:12ZLexisNexisCan The Southern Company (NYSE:SO), sustain its upward momentum this Tuesday?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

ATLANTA, Monday, May 11 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, The Southern Company closed higher the previous trading day. The Southern Company (NYSE:SO), the NYSE's 3rd largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, lifted 1.4% during its previous trading session to close at $93.10.

The Southern Company (NYSE:SO) is a leading energy provider, focusing on innovative energy infrastructure investment. Its core operations span electricity, natural gas services, and renewable energy projects, particularly in Virginia, underscoring its commitment to sustainability and grid modernization. With a strategic emphasis on utility-scale solar projects and renewable energy contracts, the company is advancing its net-zero emissions plan through strategic initiatives like battery storage, wind, and hydrogen projects. By engaging in corporate investment projects and public-private energy partnerships, The Southern Company aims to enhance grid resilience and community benefits, securing its competitive edge in the energy sector.

Wed May 6, 2026: Georgia Power Prepares Customers for Storm Season Ahead

Georgia Power is taking proactive measures to prepare its customers for the impending storm season. As severe weather is forecasted to sweep across the state, the company is urging residents to prioritize safety and readiness. With storms expected tonight and tomorrow, Georgia Power emphasizes the importance of preparation, especially as the unpredictable spring and summer weather approaches.

On the 6th of May 2026, Georgia Power announced a series of resources and safety tips designed to help customers navigate potential power outages and storms. The company is committed to ensuring that its crews are strategically positioned to respond swiftly to outages. Customers are encouraged to visit GeorgiaPower.com and follow the company on social media for real-time updates and essential safety information.

The safety tips provided by Georgia Power include stocking up on emergency supplies, charging devices, and avoiding downed power lines. Residents are reminded to never attempt to remove tree branches from power lines and to avoid standing water where downed lines may be present. Additionally, Georgia law requires drivers to move over for stopped utility vehicles, a crucial reminder for those on the road during storm recovery efforts.

Georgia Power offers several tools to keep customers informed during severe weather. The Outage Alert service allows subscribers to receive personalized notifications via text message regarding outages. The Outage & Storm Center on GeorgiaPower.com provides comprehensive resources, including an interactive Outage Map to track outages and estimated restoration times. Customers can also report outages through the website or by calling the company directly.

As the largest electric subsidiary of Southern Company (NYSE:SO), Georgia Power is dedicated to delivering reliable and affordable energy to its 2.8 million customers across the state. With a focus on customer satisfaction and safety, Georgia Power remains committed to its mission of providing clean, safe, and dependable energy solutions. For more information and updates, customers can connect with Georgia Power through its mobile app and various social media platforms.

Wed May 6, 2026: The Southern Company Drives Growth in Utility ETF Amid AI Demand Surge

The Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU) has transformed from a stable dividend provider into a surprising growth vehicle, largely due to the rising demand for power driven by artificial intelligence data centers. As of 7th May 2026, XLU has seen a remarkable 20% increase over the past year, with its year-to-date performance at around 9%. This is a significant shift for a fund that primarily invests in regulated electric and multi-utilities, including major players like NextEra Energy, Southern Company, and Constellation Energy.

Historically, the fund has been viewed as a reliable investment, generating steady quarterly dividends without interruption since 1999. The latest distribution of $0.31 was paid in March 2026. With electric utilities comprising 66% and multi-utilities making up 26% of its holdings, XLU has provided investors with a consistent return engine through allowed rates on their rate base, dividends, and capital reinvestment. Over the past five years, the fund has returned 65%, while its ten-year return stands at an impressive 161%. Yet, these returns have been overshadowed by the recent surge in AI-related power demand.

The emergence of AI has prompted a reevaluation of traditional utility demand models. The International Energy Agency has dubbed this phenomenon a 'power demand super cycle', projecting that electricity demand could more than double by 2030. Notably, American Electric Power has already secured 63 gigawatts of new load by that year. This shift in demand dynamics has led to a reappraisal of XLU's growth potential, elevating it beyond the conventional utility narrative. Analysts are now viewing XLU as a hybrid investment that combines income with growth prospects, a notable evolution for a fund typically characterized by its stability.

The interest in XLU reflects a broader trend among investors seeking to balance stability with exposure to emerging growth sectors. VettaFi's Todd Rosenbluth has highlighted this shift, noting that XLU is now seen as a thematic vehicle for grid investment, while still offering the defensive characteristics that utility investors have come to rely on. This duality makes the fund particularly appealing in a market environment where rising Treasury yields near 4% add competition for risk-averse investors.

For investors looking to diversify, XLU may serve as an effective anchor within a portfolio, representing 5-15% of holdings for those interested in both regulated utility cash flows and the burgeoning demand from data centers. However, as the market shifts, the primary risk remains the potential for paying a premium for a sector that is traditionally viewed as a bond proxy, especially as long-term interest rates continue to rise. The Southern Company, as part of this fund, stands at the intersection of traditional utility reliability and the exciting potential of AI-driven growth.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

The Southern Company, a prominent energy holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading provider of electricity and natural gas services in the southeastern United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electric power, serving approximately nine million customers through subsidiaries like Alabama Power, Georgia Power, and Mississippi Power. The company also engages in natural gas distribution and owns interests in nuclear and fossil fuel plants, emphasizing reliable and affordable energy solutions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar and wind projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, investments in modernizing transmission infrastructure and expanding natural gas operations are fueling growth, positioning the company to capitalize on the transition to a more sustainable energy landscape while maintaining operational efficiency and regulatory compliance. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

The Southern Company (NYSE:SO) is a premier energy provider, strategically positioned with a robust presence in Virginia's power generation sector. Notably, its substantial $7.1 billion capital investment in energy infrastructure underscores its commitment to economic development and community investment. Through Southern Company investment Virginia initiatives, the company is set to create 3,250 jobs, significantly boosting the local economy. Emphasizing Southern Company renewables Virginia, it also prioritizes utility-scale solar, wind projects, and battery energy storage. Their grid modernization and transmission upgrades further enhance their operational efficiency. As a leader in both Southern Company nuclear power and natural gas services Virginia, the company pioneers advanced nuclear projects and energy efficiency programs. Furthermore, Southern Company EV charging infrastructure and smart grid technology position it at the forefront of the clean energy transition. Investors can track Southern Company NYSE:SO investment news as it propels forward with innovative Southern Power Virginia projects.

urn:contentItem:6JJN-M7W3-S8VK-W29K-00000-00Market Watch Moves to The Southern Company (NYSE:SO),. Will the rise Continue?2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:11ZLexisNexisMarket Watch Moves to The Southern Company (NYSE:SO),. Will the rise Continue?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

ATLANTA, Monday, May 11 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, The Southern Company closed higher the previous trading day. The Southern Company (NYSE:SO), the NYSE's 3rd largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, lifted 1.4% during its previous trading session to close at $93.10.

The Southern Company (NYSE:SO) is a leading energy provider, focusing on innovative energy infrastructure investment. Its core operations span electricity, natural gas services, and renewable energy projects, particularly in Virginia, underscoring its commitment to sustainability and grid modernization. With a strategic emphasis on utility-scale solar projects and renewable energy contracts, the company is advancing its net-zero emissions plan through strategic initiatives like battery storage, wind, and hydrogen projects. By engaging in corporate investment projects and public-private energy partnerships, The Southern Company aims to enhance grid resilience and community benefits, securing its competitive edge in the energy sector.
