Wed May 6, 2026: Georgia Power Prepares Customers for Storm Season Ahead

Georgia Power is taking proactive measures to prepare its customers for the impending storm season. As severe weather is forecasted to sweep across the state, the company is urging residents to prioritize safety and readiness. With storms expected tonight and tomorrow, Georgia Power emphasizes the importance of preparation, especially as the unpredictable spring and summer weather approaches.

On the 6th of May 2026, Georgia Power announced a series of resources and safety tips designed to help customers navigate potential power outages and storms. The company is committed to ensuring that its crews are strategically positioned to respond swiftly to outages. Customers are encouraged to visit GeorgiaPower.com and follow the company on social media for real-time updates and essential safety information.

The safety tips provided by Georgia Power include stocking up on emergency supplies, charging devices, and avoiding downed power lines. Residents are reminded to never attempt to remove tree branches from power lines and to avoid standing water where downed lines may be present. Additionally, Georgia law requires drivers to move over for stopped utility vehicles, a crucial reminder for those on the road during storm recovery efforts.

Georgia Power offers several tools to keep customers informed during severe weather. The Outage Alert service allows subscribers to receive personalized notifications via text message regarding outages. The Outage & Storm Center on GeorgiaPower.com provides comprehensive resources, including an interactive Outage Map to track outages and estimated restoration times. Customers can also report outages through the website or by calling the company directly.

As the largest electric subsidiary of Southern Company (NYSE:SO), Georgia Power is dedicated to delivering reliable and affordable energy to its 2.8 million customers across the state. With a focus on customer satisfaction and safety, Georgia Power remains committed to its mission of providing clean, safe, and dependable energy solutions. For more information and updates, customers can connect with Georgia Power through its mobile app and various social media platforms.

Wed May 6, 2026: The Southern Company Drives Growth in Utility ETF Amid AI Demand Surge

The Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU) has transformed from a stable dividend provider into a surprising growth vehicle, largely due to the rising demand for power driven by artificial intelligence data centers. As of 7th May 2026, XLU has seen a remarkable 20% increase over the past year, with its year-to-date performance at around 9%. This is a significant shift for a fund that primarily invests in regulated electric and multi-utilities, including major players like NextEra Energy, Southern Company, and Constellation Energy.

Historically, the fund has been viewed as a reliable investment, generating steady quarterly dividends without interruption since 1999. The latest distribution of $0.31 was paid in March 2026. With electric utilities comprising 66% and multi-utilities making up 26% of its holdings, XLU has provided investors with a consistent return engine through allowed rates on their rate base, dividends, and capital reinvestment. Over the past five years, the fund has returned 65%, while its ten-year return stands at an impressive 161%. Yet, these returns have been overshadowed by the recent surge in AI-related power demand.

The emergence of AI has prompted a reevaluation of traditional utility demand models. The International Energy Agency has dubbed this phenomenon a 'power demand super cycle', projecting that electricity demand could more than double by 2030. Notably, American Electric Power has already secured 63 gigawatts of new load by that year. This shift in demand dynamics has led to a reappraisal of XLU's growth potential, elevating it beyond the conventional utility narrative. Analysts are now viewing XLU as a hybrid investment that combines income with growth prospects, a notable evolution for a fund typically characterized by its stability.

The interest in XLU reflects a broader trend among investors seeking to balance stability with exposure to emerging growth sectors. VettaFi's Todd Rosenbluth has highlighted this shift, noting that XLU is now seen as a thematic vehicle for grid investment, while still offering the defensive characteristics that utility investors have come to rely on. This duality makes the fund particularly appealing in a market environment where rising Treasury yields near 4% add competition for risk-averse investors.

For investors looking to diversify, XLU may serve as an effective anchor within a portfolio, representing 5-15% of holdings for those interested in both regulated utility cash flows and the burgeoning demand from data centers. However, as the market shifts, the primary risk remains the potential for paying a premium for a sector that is traditionally viewed as a bond proxy, especially as long-term interest rates continue to rise. The Southern Company, as part of this fund, stands at the intersection of traditional utility reliability and the exciting potential of AI-driven growth.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

The Southern Company, a prominent energy holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading provider of electricity and natural gas services in the southeastern United States. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electric power, serving approximately nine million customers through subsidiaries like Alabama Power, Georgia Power, and Mississippi Power. The company also engages in natural gas distribution and owns interests in nuclear and fossil fuel plants, emphasizing reliable and affordable energy solutions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar and wind projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, investments in modernizing transmission infrastructure and expanding natural gas operations are fueling growth, positioning the company to capitalize on the transition to a more sustainable energy landscape while maintaining operational efficiency and regulatory compliance. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

The Southern Company (NYSE:SO) is a premier energy provider, strategically positioned with a robust presence in Virginia's power generation sector. Notably, its substantial $7.1 billion capital investment in energy infrastructure underscores its commitment to economic development and community investment. Through Southern Company investment Virginia initiatives, the company is set to create 3,250 jobs, significantly boosting the local economy. Emphasizing Southern Company renewables Virginia, it also prioritizes utility-scale solar, wind projects, and battery energy storage. Their grid modernization and transmission upgrades further enhance their operational efficiency. As a leader in both Southern Company nuclear power and natural gas services Virginia, the company pioneers advanced nuclear projects and energy efficiency programs. Furthermore, Southern Company EV charging infrastructure and smart grid technology position it at the forefront of the clean energy transition. Investors can track Southern Company NYSE:SO investment news as it propels forward with innovative Southern Power Virginia projects.

value.Title: April 30, 2026: COLONIAL GROUP INC. & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-8W63-RW8H-H335-00000-00April 30, 2026: COLONIAL GROUP INC. & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:10ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 30, 2026: COLONIAL GROUP INC. & PEERS: INDUSTRY NEWS DIGEST

Colonial Group Inc. is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services through subsidiaries such as Colonial Oil and Enmarket. Serving the energy, retail, and transportation sectors, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. Competing with major infrastructure firms like Kinder Morgan, the company leverages its regional expertise and integrated services. It recently expanded its renewable fuel offerings to align with global sustainability trends and continues to emphasize safety, community engagement, and investments in green energy and logistics technology.

In related industry developments, Australian wealth management peer Colonial First State announced a strategic investment in Oracle's AI-powered Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its technological framework and improve operational efficiency. Managing AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians as of March 24, 2026, the firm is consolidating its core systems into a unified platform to accelerate innovation. Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer at Colonial First State, noted that modernizing core systems gives their people more time to focus on supporting members. The transition incorporates Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management, Enterprise Resource Planning, and Enterprise Performance Management, which Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, stated will enhance operational agility and data-driven insights.

Within the financial sector, 1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. received shareholder approval on February 11, 2026, for a merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. With over 80% of outstanding shares represented at a special meeting, the merger proposal passed with over 99% of the votes cast in favor. Rory G. Ritrievi, Chair, President, and CEO of Mid Penn Bancorp, acknowledged the tremendous level of support for the transaction, while 1st Colonial Bancorp's President and CEO, Robert White, indicated that the merger will enhance their ability to serve customers. Upon completion, Mid Penn Bancorp is projected to hold approximately $7.5 billion in assets as of March 31, 2026, extending its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area, Southeastern Pennsylvania, and Southern New Jersey.

Meanwhile, in the energy infrastructure space, competitor Kinder Morgan, Inc. and Phillips 66 are advancing the Western Gateway Pipeline project after securing necessary long-term shipper commitments by April 20, 2026. This newly proposed refined products pipeline system is designed to supply fuel from the Midwest and Gulf Coast directly to Phoenix, Arizona, and various markets in California. The initial open season concluded on December 23, 2025, generating substantial market interest, which prompted the companies to schedule a subsequent open season for January 2026. The project will feature a new pipeline from Borger, Texas, to Phoenix, alongside the reversal of existing infrastructure, including Kinder Morgan's SFPP pipeline system between Colton and Phoenix, to facilitate east-to-west fuel transportation. The Western Gateway Pipeline is expected to commence service in mid-2029, creating a major new fuel supply route to the U.S. West Coast.

urn:contentItem:6JJG-GS43-RTYP-73VK-00000-00Wood Bioenergy Conference Reveals Industry That’s Far From Reaching Potential2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:11ZLexisNexisNEWSWood Bioenergy Conference Reveals Industry That’s Far From Reaching PotentialHB Media

The message out of the ninth Wood Bioenergy Conference & Expo was that an industry that was once dominated by the discussion of industrial wood pellets has transformed into something larger, with opportunities bursting at the seams, if only the industry can see them through. Perhaps no comment expressed the potential of the industry than that of...

The message out of the ninth Wood Bioenergy Conference & Expo was that an industry that was once dominated by the discussion of industrial wood pellets has transformed into something larger, with opportunities bursting at the seams, if only the industry ca...

The message out of the ninth Wood Bioenergy Conference & Expo was that an industry that was once dominated by the discussion of industrial wood pellets has transformed into something larger, with opportunities bursting at the seams, if only the industry can see them through. Perhaps no comment expressed the potential of the industry than that of Dru Preston, staff forester with the Georgia Forestry Commission, who said that a single data center requiring 1.2 gigawatts (1,200 megawatt) could be supplied by 12 100 MW biomass power plants using 1.2 million tons of wood chips annually. In a state where the logging supply chains ranks has taken a super blow due to the loss of 8.3 million tons of timber market because of regional pulp mill closures (approximately 290,000 truckloads of mostly pulpwood timber), the potential biomass for data centers would be a welcome reprieve, with a single 100 MW plant requiring 75 jobs at the facility, 20 logging and trucking crews, numerous forestry and fiber supply jobs, and trickling down the supply chains to additional labor for site prep and seedlings crews—not to mention serving as a chips byproduct market for the abundance of sawmills in the region. And if that isn’t potential enough, what about the 520 million green tons of biomass that would be required to transform the nation’s annual conventional jet fuel into sustainable aviation fuel (SAF)? Such was the sky’s-the-limit nature of the Wood Bioenergy Conference & Expo, held April 14-15 at the Omni Atlanta Hotel at Centennial Park in downtown Atlanta. Hosted by Wood Bioenergy magazine, the event attracted nearly 200 participants, featured 22 speakers and 43 exhibitors in the Grand Ballroom North of the Omni. Not that the conference overlooked the longstanding industrial wood pellets business. Indeed, the conference served as kind of a “coming out party” for Enviva, the world’s largest producer of industrial wood pellets, which, while maintaining its status as number one, has fought through tough times in the past two years. But even Scott Bax, executive vice president and COO of Enviva, spoke on the bigger potential in his talk, “From Biomass to Bioenergy and Beyond.” Bax noted that Enviva and the global pellet industry created the first large-scale market for woody biomass as a renewable carbon feedstock; but it’s no longer defined by a single product, “rather a versatile renewable carbon feedstock…replacing fossil electricity with renewable power and replacing fossil carbon with renewable carbon,” with potential in biocoal, biochar, black pellets and SAF. Bax said Enviva continues to operate 10 pellet production plants with a run-rate of 6.5 million metric tons, including Enviva’s newest plant that has started up in Epes, Ala. Day two of the conference included a tour of the Hazlehurst Wood Pellets plant in Hazlehurst, Ga., one of the first large scale industrial wood pellet producers in the world, and of the new Telfair Forest Products torrefaction facility in Lumber City, Ga.
